turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <!-- Background -->
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="#f7f7f8" />

  <!-- Clock (top) -->
  <g transform="translate(0,0)">
    <circle cx="256" cy="100" r="64" fill="#fffef8" stroke="#3b3b3b" stroke-width="6" />
    <!-- ticks -->
    <line x1="256" y1="36" x2="256" y2="52" stroke="#3b3b3b" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <line x1="320" y1="100" x2="304" y2="100" stroke="#3b3b3b" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <line x1="256" y1="164" x2="256" y2="148" stroke="#3b3b3b" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <line x1="192" y1="100" x2="208" y2="100" stroke="#3b3b3b" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <!-- hands -->
    <line x1="256" y1="100" x2="296" y2="76" stroke="#2b2b2b" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <line x1="256" y1="100" x2="236" y2="124" stroke="#2b2b2b" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <circle cx="256" cy="100" r="6" fill="#2b2b2b" />
  </g>

  <!-- Hourglass (center) -->
  <g>
    <!-- frame bars -->
    <rect x="150" y="188" width="212" height="14" rx="6" fill="#8b6b3a" />
    <rect x="150" y="390" width="212" height="14" rx="6" fill="#8b6b3a" />
    <!-- vertical posts -->
    <rect x="166" y="200" width="12" height="190" rx="6" fill="#6b4e2d" />
    <rect x="334" y="200" width="12" height="190" rx="6" fill="#6b4e2d" />

    <!-- glass outline (two triangles forming hourglass) -->
    <polygon points="176,220 336,220 256,300" fill="#eaf6ff" fill-opacity="0.6" stroke="#4b8bbf" stroke-width="4" stroke-linejoin="round" />
    <polygon points="256,300 336,380 176,380" fill="#eaf6ff" fill-opacity="0.6" stroke="#4b8bbf" stroke-width="4" stroke-linejoin="round" />

    <!-- top remaining sand (small wedge) -->
    <polygon points="216,230 296,230 256,264" fill="#e2b07a" />
    <!-- falling sand grain -->
    <circle cx="256" cy="285" r="4" fill="#e2b07a" />
    <!-- bottom sand pile -->
    <path d="M216 360 C236 345, 276 345, 296 360 C276 372, 236 372, 216 360 Z" fill="#e2b07a" />

    <!-- subtle inner glass highlight -->
    <path d="M184 224 C222 260, 222 340, 184 368" fill="none" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-opacity="0.35" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round" />
  </g>

</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.content:
{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <!-- Background -->
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="#f7f7f8" />

  <!-- Clock (top) -->
  <g transform="translate(0,0)">
    <circle cx="256" cy="100" r="64" fill="#fffef8" stroke="#3b3b3b" stroke-width="6" />
    <!-- ticks -->
    <line x1="256" y1="36" x2="256" y2="52" stroke="#3b3b3b" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <line x1="320" y1="100" x2="304" y2="100" stroke="#3b3b3b" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <line x1="256" y1="164" x2="256" y2="148" stroke="#3b3b3b" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <line x1="192" y1="100" x2="208" y2="100" stroke="#3b3b3b" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <!-- hands -->
    <line x1="256" y1="100" x2="296" y2="76" stroke="#2b2b2b" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <line x1="256" y1="100" x2="236" y2="124" stroke="#2b2b2b" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <circle cx="256" cy="100" r="6" fill="#2b2b2b" />
  </g>

  <!-- Hourglass (center) -->
  <g>
    <!-- frame bars -->
    <rect x="150" y="188" width="212" height="14" rx="6" fill="#8b6b3a" />
    <rect x="150" y="390" width="212" height="14" rx="6" fill="#8b6b3a" />
    <!-- vertical posts -->
    <rect x="166" y="200" width="12" height="190" rx="6" fill="#6b4e2d" />
    <rect x="334" y="200" width="12" height="190" rx="6" fill="#6b4e2d" />

    <!-- glass outline (two triangles forming hourglass) -->
    <polygon points="176,220 336,220 256,300" fill="#eaf6ff" fill-opacity="0.6" stroke="#4b8bbf" stroke-width="4" stroke-linejoin="round" />
    <polygon points="256,300 336,380 176,380" fill="#eaf6ff" fill-opacity="0.6" stroke="#4b8bbf" stroke-width="4" stroke-linejoin="round" />

    <!-- top remaining sand (small wedge) -->
    <polygon points="216,230 296,230 256,264" fill="#e2b07a" />
    <!-- falling sand grain -->
    <circle cx="256" cy="285" r="4" fill="#e2b07a" />
    <!-- bottom sand pile -->
    <path d="M216 360 C236 345, 276 345, 296 360 C276 372, 236 372, 216 360 Z" fill="#e2b07a" />

    <!-- subtle inner glass highlight -->
    <path d="M184 224 C222 260, 222 340, 184 368" fill="none" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-opacity="0.35" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round" />
  </g>

</svg>"
}
message.reasoning:
**Creating time visuals with SVG**

I'm working on drawing "time" using only SVG visuals, without any text or numbers. I'm considering a simple image, like a clock, hourglass, or calendar. An analog clock face would work, possibly combined with an hourglass for a unique touch. I want to keep it simple, with some shapes, and the motion impression even if it's static. The canvas will be 512x512, aiming for a clean design with an overlapping hourglass and clock.**Crafting SVG for time representation**

I'm using simple shapes and paths for my SVG, considering colors like sand and a glass gradient. The design will include a background rectangle with a subtle gradient. I'll draw a clock circle at the top center and mark ticks without using numbers. The clock hands will be simple rectangles or lines, indicating time. Below, I'll center an hourglass with path shapes. I need to ensure everything is simple and valid for JSON formatting, keeping it neat.**Creating structured SVG for hourglass**

I'm working on producing a clean SVG while keeping it within valid JSON format by using double quotes and escaping internal quotes where needed. For the hourglass, I've decided on using two triangles for simplicity, pointing at each other to create the shape. The top triangle will be tan to represent sand, and the bottom will depict the sand pile. I’ve laid out the hourglass to be centered at (256, 330), ensuring the dimensions and points work well together to avoid intersection.**Designing the hourglass and clock**

I'm designing the top sand area of the hourglass as a smaller triangle, showing sand falling, and maybe using a small polygon to represent the sand funnel. The bottom sand pile will be a polygon at the base of the triangle. I’ll add horizontal bars for the frame and vertical rods for support. For the clock, I'll center a circle with hands at specific angles, using lines rather than rectangles for simplicity. I'll ensure to include ticks without any numbers for a clean design.**Refining SVG design**

I'm deciding to keep the background white and avoid drop shadows. The SVG string needs to be constructed carefully, making sure to escape double quotes for proper JSON formatting. For the clock, I'm setting the circle's properties and positioning ticks accurately within the circle. The hands will pivot correctly, with the hour hand at 135 degrees pointing down-left. The hourglass will consist of polygons with a light blue fill, and I want to represent falling sand simply. I'll craft the final JSON for the SVG content to ensure it’s readable and avoids complexity.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}

response.rawBody: {"id":"gen-1772255621-1BedQuXq9KqhgbseLjmx","provider":"Azure","model":"openai/gpt-5-mini","object":"chat.completion","created":1772255621,"choices":[{"logprobs":null,"finish_reason":"stop","native_finish_reason":"completed","index":0,"message":{"role":"assistant","content":"{ \"guesses\": [\"time\", \"hourglass\", \"clock\", \"timer\", \"sand\", \"watch\", \"hour\", \"minute\", \"seconds\", \"timepiece\", \"duration\", \"countdown\", \"stopwatch\", \"timing\", \"schedule\", \"deadline\", \"moment\", \"period\", \"hourhand\", \"hand\"] }","refusal":null,"reasoning":"**Generating time-related guesses**\n\nI need to come up with 20 one-word guesses related to the image of a round analog clock and an hourglass. The highest confidence guess is likely \"time.\" Other possibilities include \"clock,\" \"hourglass,\" \"sand,\" \"timer,\" \"minutes,\" \"hours,\" \"chronometer,\" and \"deadline.\" I’ll focus on everyday vocabulary to ensure clarity. I need to avoid spaces, punctuation, or numbers, and make sure each guess is unique. It’s a bit tricky to find the right balance!**Creating a confidence-based list**\n\nI’m putting together a list of 20 time-related words, sorting them by confidence. message.reasoning:
**Generating time-related guesses**

I need to come up with 20 one-word guesses related to the image of a round analog clock and an hourglass. The highest confidence guess is likely "time." Other possibilities include "clock," "hourglass," "sand," "timer," "minutes," "hours," "chronometer," and "deadline." I’ll focus on everyday vocabulary to ensure clarity. I need to avoid spaces, punctuation, or numbers, and make sure each guess is unique. It’s a bit tricky to find the right balance!**Creating a confidence-based list**

I’m putting together a list of 20 time-related words, sorting them by confidence. The top guesses are “time,” “hourglass,” “clock,” “timer,” and “sand.” I want to ensure all words are relevant and unique. It’s good to avoid duplicates and keep it to one word with no spaces or punctuation. I'm including a mix of terms like "duration," "deadline," and "schedule" while double-checking if "seconds" is acceptable as plural. Everything seems to fit together nicely!**Finalizing the guesses**

I’m thinking about whether to use "second" in the singular, but both "second" and "seconds" might work. I’ll avoid any repeats, which is crucial. Now, I need to return the guesses in JSON format with a field labeled "guesses" containing an array of 20 strings. It’s also important to confirm I'm using US English, but I realize the term "behaviour" isn’t relevant here. Let’s finalize this and produce the final output now!
